msgs.text:
Turn 83 — final appendix contract, with proof boundary frozen I re-read the attached turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md, mega_prompt.md, and problem_statement.md only. I did not use File Library material. The current state is not an obstruction search: Turn 82 froze the conclusion as a certificate theorem, with the remaining work only the presentation of verifier artifacts, not new mathematics. The attached history records the final target as a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, which descends to ΓS≅GΓH.The correct Turn 83 move is therefore to state the final appendix contract cleanly and prevent drift back into a particular Magnus layer, coefficient quotient, or exceptional subcase.

Certificate Appendix Contract Theorem

There exists a continuous automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G⟶F=P⋊G over G such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Consequently,ΓS≅GΓH.

Indeed, since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, the equality Φ(rS−1)=rH implies Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

Thus Φ descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,which is exactlyΓS≅GΓH.

This matches the required conditionπH∘Φ=πS.The attached history explicitly records this stronger F-level statement and the quotient descent.

Certificate 0 — fixed orientation

Use the source orientationρS:=rS−1.Every finite and profinite Nielsen substitution is required to sendρS⟼rH.This removes sign ambiguity. It is harmless because⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F=⟨⟨rS−1⟩⟩F.The final history repeatedly fixes this orientation before compactness.

Certificate A — H1

Nielsen equivalenceLetΛ=Z2 [[G]].

With the right Λ-basis X0,X1 of Pab, the linearized rows for the fixed source orientation arewS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E).

The sweep constructs

U∈GL2(Λ)such thatwSU=wH.

On the dangerous Ef-piece, with s=σ2, the rows are (s+3,−2),(s2+1,s−1).

Using the right-row basis-action convention, takeU0(e0)=e0 (s−1)+e1 (s−2),U0(e1)=e0+2e1.

Equivalently, in ordinary displayed matrix form,U0=( s−1s−212).Then (s+3,−2)U0=(s2+1,s−1),and det(U0)=2(s−1)−(s−2)=s,which is a unit. The other idempotent pieces are handled by swaps or unit-controlled matrices, so the first Magnus layer is invertible globally. The attached history flags this right-row convention as part of the frozen proof boundary.

Certificate B — primitive-boundary verifier

In the residual mod-2 Magnus algebra, defineLn=Primn(F2⟨A,B⟩)using the reduced coproduct. The verifier computesdimL1,…,dimL10=2,3,2,6,6,11,18,36,56,105.

The ordinary correction boundary is∂d:Ld⊕Ld→Ld+1,(u,v)↦[A,u]+[B,v],with [X,Y]=XY+YX in characteristic 2.The recorded verifier output includes the needed boundary ranks and secondary quotient ranks through d=10, including the tableddimLd rank∂d dimker∂d46665693611184718306836561695699131010518624andd rankθd0dimHd(2)45153364776128152191343102481.These are the primitive-boundary ranks used throughout the residual and coefficient arguments.

Certificate C — residual restricted-Lie induction

On the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 piece, the equation isS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2,whereS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q.If the first nonzero residual error has degree n, it is primitive, hence lies in Ln. The correction boundary is∂n−1:Ln−1⊕Ln−1→Ln.

The residual quotient has the uniform formLn/im∂n−1≅{0,Ln/2[2],n odd,n even.

The sweep provesCm=Lm (m≥5).Thus every odd layer is an ordinary boundary, and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression

W↦W[2].Therefore the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes to all orders.

Certificate D — finite nonlinear blocks The finite nonlinear block is frozen as four certificates.

BlockD.1D.2D.3D.4 content ker∂8↠L5[2]residual degree-16 λ-toggleA2=R/(4,t),d=4,μ4 -toggled=8 quotient witness certificate six displayed Bockstein witnessesY0↦Y0Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4)))rankD5,6,7=60, rank(D5,6,7,μ4B4)=61θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2], λ((B8)[2])=1.The last previously open executable item was D.3; the attached history records that the verifier computed rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7,μ4B4)=61.

Since H4(2)is one-dimensional, this proves that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed μ4=1 scalar to μ4=0.

The d=8 quotient witness is also frozen: (0,B8)∈ker([A,−]+[B,−]), andθ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2].

Sinceλ((B8)[2])=1,the residual codimension-one miss disappears after quotienting by imθ80.

Certificate E — normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction

On the Ef-coefficient ring,

R=EfΛ≅Z2 [[t]],t=s−1.

The raw local row is(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t).

Normalize it exactly by N(a,b)=(a−(s+1)b,b).

Since(s2+1)−(s+1)(s−1)=2,the normalized row is (2,t).Thus the coefficient problem is governed by the regular Koszul row (2,t), not by the raw (2+2t+t2,t).The first t-layer is contractible. The first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfyBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4),with the base range 4≤d≤8 supplied by D.1–D.4 and the range d≥9 supplied by the coefficient-safe induction.For higher (2,t)-adic layers, actual pro-2 representatives produce Witt cascades, not a single naive Frobenius term. Ordered by Magnus root degree first, every forced tail lands later in the induction. Koszul syzygies slide β-powers to pure α-powers; pure α-multiples are killed by the first 2-Bockstein maps; coefficient-free terms fall back into the closed residual tower. Hence every finite Ef-local coefficient obstruction is killable.

Certificate F — finite filtered Nielsen compactnessLet

M=Λ⟨⟨X0,X1⟩⟩,I=(X0,X1).

For a degree cutoff N and open two-sided ideal J⊂Λ, definePN,J:=P∩(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is a finite 2-group andN,J⋂PN,J=1.

Define SN,J to be the finite set of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data(y0,y1,cσ,cτ)∈(P/PN,J)4 such that:the first Magnus layer of y0,y1 is UmodJ,cσ,cτ satisfy the finite cocycle relation induced by τσ=τ2,the induced twisted substitution sends ρS=rS−1 to rH (modPN,J).Each datum is an actual finite over-G automorphism: the first layer is invertible, so the finite Frattini criterion applies to the finite 2-group P/PN,J.

Certificates A–E implySN,J=∅ for every finite truncation. The sets SN,J are finite and functorial under refinement. Compactness givesN,JlimSN,J=∅.A compatible inverse-limit point gives a continuous filtered Nielsen automorphism α:P→P and a continuous cocycle c:G→P.

Together they define an over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G→F=P⋊G,Φ(pg)=α(p)c(g)g.

At every finite level,Φ(ρS)≡rH (modPN,J).

Intersecting over all N,J givesΦ(rS−1)=rH inside F. Therefore Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,and the descended isomorphism satisfiesΓS≅GΓH.This finite filtered Nielsen compactness step is the final global mechanism, avoiding both the false degree-only finite quotient and any Hopficity shortcut for the infinite pro-2 kernel.

Turn 83 audit No new Magnus layer, coefficient quotient, or exceptional finite case should be opened. The structural pattern has already closed the problem uniformly: the H1 row equivalence fixes the first layer; non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by Cm=Lm; the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to (2,t) and the Koszul–Witt induction; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness gives the actual over-G automorphism.The only remaining work for a standalone paper is presentation-level reproducibility: attach verifier scripts, pivot tables, or explicit matrices for the certificate blocks, especially D.1, D.2, and the D.3 stack-rank 60→61. The attached history explicitly treats this as an external appendix issue, not a structural mathematical gap.

ESTABLISHED: The final certificate theorem is frozen. Certificates 0–F yield a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, hence ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The only remaining gap is external manuscript reproducibility: include the verifier scripts, matrices, or pivot tables supporting Certificates B–D.

NEXT: Finalize the manuscript appendix as a checkable artifact; do not reopen any obstruction layer, coefficient ghost, or exceptional subcase.[TURN83-FINAL-CERTIFICATE-APPENDIX-CONTRACT-FROZEN]
